title: BitSight API - Authentication And Request Conventions

confirmed_facts.fact: BitSight API authentication uses HTTP Basic Authentication with the API token as the username and an empty password.
All BitSight API requests must be made over HTTPS; HTTP requests fail.
The BitSight API is REST-oriented and returns JSON by default for responses and errors, with XML available when specifically requested.
The main ratings API base URL is under https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v... and some specialized feeds, such as infection alerts, use separate hostnames.
BitSight supports both company API tokens and user API tokens, with company tokens preferred for durable integrations because they are not tied to a single user account.

implementation_notes: Represent the token as a secret but send it in the Basic Auth username position with a blank password.
Prefer company tokens for long-lived service integrations so user offboarding does not break automation.
Check endpoint-specific docs for any separate token families or alternate hosts before assuming the ratings API hostname applies everywhere.
For ratings-centric work, treat https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v1/current-ratings as the primary current-state security-rating surface.
caveats: Not every BitSight API uses the same hostname; specialized APIs can have separate domains and token requirements.
Access to some datasets and endpoints is role- or license-dependent.
